cire: noun. A fabric with a glazed finish.
cis: symbol. The function c i s (x) = cos ⁡ x + i sin ⁡ x {\displaystyle \mathrm {cis} (x)=\cos x+i\sin x}.
cist: noun. A small receptacle for sacred utensils carried in festivals in Ancient Greece.
cite: verb. To quote; to repeat, as a passage from a book, or the words of another.
crest: noun. The summit of a hill or mountain ridge.
cries: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of cry
critter: noun. A creature, and specifically, an animal or something animal-like.
err: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Erre.
errs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of err
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
etic: adjective. Of or pertaining to analysis of a culture from a perspective situated outside all cultures.
ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
ires: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ire
its: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Itsekiri.
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
reis: noun. The holder of an Ottoman military rank akin to that of naval captain.
rest: noun. Relief from work or activity by sleeping; sleep.
restrict: verb. To restrain within boundaries; to limit; to confine
ret: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Retta.
rets: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ret
rice: noun. Cereal plants, Oryza sativa of the grass family whose seeds are used as food.
ricer: noun. A person, especially a Native American, who cultivates and harvests rice.
rise: verb. To move, or appear to move, physically upwards relative to the ground.
riser: noun. Someone or something which rises.
sec: symbol. Symbol of the trigonometric function secant.
sect: noun. An offshoot of a larger religion or denomination.
sei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seri.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
set: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sentani.
sett: noun. The system of tunnels that is the home of a badger.
sic: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Malinguat.
sir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siri.
sire: noun. A lord, master, or other person in authority, most commonly used vocatively: formerly in speaking to elders and superiors, later only
sit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Sino-Tibetan languages.
site: noun. The place where anything is fixed; situation; local position
sitter: noun. Someone who sits, e.g. for a portrait.
sri: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siriano.
steric: adjective. Relating to or involving the arrangement of atoms in space.
stet: noun. A symbol used by proofreaders and typesetters to indicate that a word or phrase that was crossed out or changed should remain as it
stir: verb. To disturb the relative position of the particles (of a liquid or similar) by passing an object through it.
stricter: adjective. comparative form of strict: more strict
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
tics: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of tic
tire: verb. To become sleepy or weary.
tis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masadiit Itneg.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
titer: noun. The concentration of a substance as determined by titration.
titre: noun. The strength or concentration of a solution that has been determined by titration.
tret: noun. An allowance to purchasers, for waste or refuse matter, of four pounds on every 104 pounds of suttle weight, or weight after the tare
trice: verb. To pull, to pull out or away, to pull sharply.
trier: noun. One who tries; one who makes experiments or examines anything by a test or standard.
trite: adjective. used so many times that it is commonplace, or no longer interesting or effective; worn out, hackneyed.
triter: adjective. comparative form of trite: more trite
